name: GPT-5.6 Terra
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer); mid 'durable capability tier' of the GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna family, billed as balancing intelligence and cost
note: Mid tier of the three-tier GPT-5.6 family (GA July 9, 2026 alongside Sol and Luna); OpenAI positions the tiers as 'durable capability tiers that can advance on their own cadence' — Terra is the balanced everyday model between flagship Sol and high-volume Luna. Same 1.05M context, 128K max output and Feb 2026 knowledge cutoff as Sol, at half Sol's price ($2.50/$15 vs $5/$30 per MTok). Distinct model tier, not a reasoning-effort configuration of Sol (each tier separately exposes its own effort settings).

results.notes: SWE-Bench Pro, GPT-5.6 Terra, OpenAI launch comparison table (Sol 64.6 / Terra 63.4 / Luna 62.7); per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs), corroborated by BenchLM's Terra tracker (63.4%).

Terminal-Bench 2.1, GPT-5.6 Terra standard configuration, OpenAI launch table; per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs), corroborated by BenchLM (87.4%). Artificial Analysis's own independent Terminal-Bench 2.1 run for Terra reads 88%.
BrowseComp, GPT-5.6 Terra, OpenAI launch table; per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs), corroborated by BenchLM (87.5%).
Agents' Last Exam, GPT-5.6 Terra, OpenAI launch table (Sol 52.7 / Terra 50.4 / Luna 50.3); per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs).
FrontierMath Tier 1-3 v2, GPT-5.6 Terra, OpenAI launch table; per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs), corroborated by BenchLM (84.9%).
FrontierMath Tier 4 v2, GPT-5.6 Terra, OpenAI launch table; per kingy.ai's launch-table digest (kingy.ai/blog/gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-benchmarks-specs), corroborated by BenchLM (68.3%).
GPQA Diamond, GPT-5.6 Terra, BenchLM tracker snapshot (AA's own AA-GPQA-Diamond run reads 92.5%). Same BenchLM provenance as the Sol tier's 94.6% row.

GDPval-AA v2 (Elo) for GPT-5.6 Terra = 1593, from MarkTechPost's direct report of the GPT-5.6 launch comparison table — the same source and table as the Sol tier's 1747.8 row, which explicitly lists Terra 1593 and Luna 1591.8. GDPval-AA is Artificial Analysis's own independent methodology, hence 'independent'.

CyberGym, GPT-5.6 Terra = 81.8%, BenchLM tracker snapshot — same third-party leaderboard provenance as the Sol tier's 84.5% row (OpenAI's own launch materials use a different internal CTF suite, not CyberGym).
MMMU-Pro, GPT-5.6 Terra, BenchLM tracker snapshot (82.0% with Python tool).
Humanity's Last Exam, GPT-5.6 Terra = 41.8%, BenchLM's snapshot of Artificial Analysis's independent AA-HLE run — same AA methodology as the Sol tier's 47.2% row.
τ³-Banking (Artificial Analysis methodology), GPT-5.6 Terra = 31.8%, BenchLM tracker snapshot.
IFBench (Artificial Analysis run), GPT-5.6 Terra = 71.2%, BenchLM tracker snapshot.

verificationNotes:
Existence, tier positioning, $2.50/$15 pricing, 1.05M context, 128K max output, Feb 16 2026 knowledge cutoff, and text+vision(input) modalities all confirmed directly from OpenAI's official model docs page (developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.6-terra, fetched 2026-07-24); pricing independently corroborated by finout.io, eesel.ai and aipricing.guru tier-pricing explainers. Added to the corpus as part of the GPT-5.6 tier split; the sibling flagship Sol tier was already tracked (formerly under the bare gpt-5-6 slug).

2026-07-24 TIER SPLIT / FULL INGESTION: promoted to a full sibling model of Sol and Luna (config flags true/true — three distinct capability-tier models like Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, not an effort cohort). Ingested 13 further benchmark rows: six self-reported from OpenAI's launch comparison table (via kingy.ai's digest, cross-corroborated by BenchLM's independent tracker where available — every overlapping value matched exactly) and seven independent (BenchLM tracker snapshots incl. AA-run figures, plus the MarkTechPost-reported GDPval-AA launch table). Deliberately not mapped for lack of a taxonomy slug: OSWorld 2.0 (50.2%), ARC-AGI-3 (0.8%), DeepSWE (69.6%), Toolathlon (53.1%), τ²-Bench 86.3% (variant unspecified), Terminal-Bench Hard (57.6%), FrontierCode/CursorBench/SciCode/CritPt/HealthBench/Omniscience/ExploitBench/ExploitGym, BenchCAD, MRCR/GraphWalks, and all AA composite indices.
